text.title: Prompt Changelog Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules every prompt edit must satisfy. Pre-commit hooks enforce them; the discipline only works if it is mechanical.

rule.statement: Each prompt lives in its own file (e.g. `prompts/classify-intent.txt`) or its own keyed entry in a YAML registry (`prompts.yaml` with prompt_id keys). Prompts MUST NOT be inlined into source code as string literals or f-strings. A grep for triple-quoted multiline strings in source files surfaces this anti-pattern.

rationale.source: prompt-management research; observability patterns from LMOps tooling

rule.rationale: Inline prompts cannot be hashed, cannot be diffed cleanly, and force regression analysis through git-blame on source files that change for unrelated reasons. Externalising prompts is the prerequisite for any changelog discipline.

example.title: Inline prompt anti-pattern
Externalised prompt
example.code:
PROMPT = load_prompt("classify-intent") # reads prompts/classify-intent.txt
def classify_intent(user_msg): response = anthropic_client.messages.create( model="claude-opus-4-7-1m", messages=[{"role": "user", "content": PROMPT.format(user_msg=user_msg)}], ) return response.content[0].text

rule.statement: Pre-commit hook computes a stable content hash (SHA-256 truncated to 16 chars, computed after normalising whitespace and stripping comments) of every prompt that changed in the commit. The hash is written into the changelog entry. Commits without an up-to-date hash on every changed prompt are rejected.

rationale.source: content-addressable identity in MLOps; debugging-by-hash patterns

rule.rationale: A stable hash lets observability platforms (logs, eval runs, traces) tag each LLM call with the exact prompt version. When a regression surfaces, the hash directly identifies the offending prompt without needing the git SHA.

rule.statement: Each changelog entry has: a one-sentence `intent` field (what this edit is trying to achieve), and an `eval_delta` field with before/after scores on at least one named eval. Eval delta is computed by the CI eval runner, not hand-typed. Entries missing either field are rejected at PR review.
rule.rationale: Intent without eval is wishful thinking; eval without intent loses the "why" that future readers need. Together they form the minimum useful record. CI-computed eval prevents the failure mode where the engineer claims an improvement without running the suite.

example.title: Changelog entry
example.code: ## 2026-05-19 — prompts/classify-intent.txt hash: a7b3f2c189de4501 prev_hash: 4419c2d80f8a8d2b intent: reduce false positives on multi-language refund requests eval_delta: refund_intent_classifier_eval_v3: accuracy: 0.876 -> 0.912 (+3.6 pp) false_positive_rate: 0.064 -> 0.041 (-2.3 pp) rollout: canary-10pct (week 1) then 100pct (week 2 if false_positive_rate stable) rollback_hash: 4419c2d80f8a8d2b authors: [ruslan, alex]

rule.statement: Every changelog entry includes a `rollback_hash` pointing to the immediately previous prompt-hash, regardless of how confident the team is in the new edit. The `prompt-rollback.py` script reads the changelog to find the rollback target; if the field is missing, rollback is manual and slow.

rationale.source: incident-response data on prompt regressions; MTTR research

rule.rationale: A regression discovered at 3 AM is not the time to figure out what the previous prompt was. The rollback_hash field turns rollback into a one-line command: `prompt-rollback classify-intent --to 4419c2d80f8a8d2b`. MTTR for prompt regressions drops from hours to minutes.

rule.statement: Each production deploy produces a `deploy-summary.md` listing every prompt change in the release with its hash, eval delta, and rollout strategy. The summary is committed to the release branch and posted to the team channel. Deploys without a summary are blocked at the release gate.
rule.rationale: Without a per-deploy summary, the team cannot correlate a customer-visible regression to a specific release. The summary is also the artifact that on-call reads first when triaging a quality alert.

list.item: Collapse runs of whitespace inside lines to a single space
Strip lines that consist entirely of #-comments
Preserve the order of all non-comment lines
Use SHA-256 truncated to 16 lowercase hex chars
Do NOT lowercase the prompt text; case is semantically meaningful
